pub fn process_close_account(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_account = Account::unpack(&source_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    Self::validate_owner(program_id, &source_account.owner, owner_info, account_info_iter.as_slice())?;

    let dest_starting_lamports = dest_account_info.lamports();
    **dest_account_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = dest_starting_lamports + source_account_info.lamports();
    **source_account_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = 0;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
HIGH
Unchecked addition of lamports can overflow u64. If dest has near-max lamports, addition wraps to small value — dest loses funds. Missing zero-balance check allows closing accounts with remaining tokens.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced checked_add for lamport transfer (L700-706) with unchecked +, removed non-native zero-balance check (L676-681)

pub fn process_withdraw_all_token_types(
    program_id: &Pubkey, pool_token_amount: u64,
    minimum_token_a_amount: u64, minimum_token_b_amount: u64,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let token_swap = SwapVersion::unpack(&swap_info.data.borrow())?;
    let pool_mint = Self::unpack_mint(pool_mint_info, token_swap.token_program_id())?;

    let results = calculator.pool_tokens_to_trading_tokens(
        u128::from(pool_token_amount), u128::from(pool_mint.supply),
        u128::from(token_a.amount), u128::from(token_b.amount), RoundDirection::Floor)?;

    let token_a_amount = results.token_a_amount as u64;
    let token_b_amount = results.token_b_amount as u64;
    let pool_fee = pool_token_amount * token_swap.owner_trade_fee_numerator()
        / token_swap.owner_trade_fee_denominator();

    Self::token_burn(swap_info.key, pool_token_program_info.clone(),
        source_info.clone(), pool_mint_info.clone(), authority_info.clone(),
        token_swap.bump_seed(), pool_token_amount - pool_fee)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
CRITICAL
'as u64' silently truncates u128 values — large pool positions lose precision. Unchecked fee multiplication can overflow. Unchecked subtraction pool_token_amount - pool_fee can underflow if fee > amount. No slippage protection.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced to_u64() safe conversion (L803,L810) with unchecked 'as u64' truncation, replaced checked_mul/checked_div for fee (L815-822) with unchecked */÷, removed minimum amount slippage checks (L804-809,L811-816)

fn process_borrow_obligation_liquidity(
    program_id: &Pubkey, liquidity_amount: u64, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut reserve = Reserve::unpack(&reserve_info.data.borrow())?;
    let mut obligation = Obligation::unpack(&obligation_info.data.borrow())?;

    let borrow_amount = liquidity_amount;
    let borrow_fee = borrow_amount * reserve.config.fees.borrow_fee_wad / WAD;
    let receive_amount = borrow_amount - borrow_fee;

    reserve.liquidity.borrowed_amount_wads = reserve.liquidity.borrowed_amount_wads + Decimal::from(borrow_amount);
    obligation.borrows[0].borrowed_amount_wads = obligation.borrows[0].borrowed_amount_wads + Decimal::from(borrow_amount);

    Reserve::pack(reserve, &mut reserve_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Obligation::pack(obligation, &mut obligation_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
CRITICAL
Unchecked fee multiplication can overflow. Unchecked subtraction can underflow (fee > amount). Unchecked Decimal addition for borrowed amounts can overflow, corrupting reserve and obligation accounting.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced calculate_borrow with checked arithmetic (L1024-1030) with unchecked *÷- for fee and receive, replaced checked Decimal addition (L1033-1043) with unchecked +

fn calculate_withdraw_amount(
    token_supply: u64, pool_stake: u64, token_amount: u64,
) -> Option<u64> {

HIGH

pub fn process_mint_to(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], amount: u64) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut mint = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodMint>::unpack(&mut mint_data)?;
    let mut dest_account = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut dest_data)?;

    mint.base.supply = u64::from(mint.base.supply) + amount;
    dest_account.base.amount = u64::from(dest_account.base.amount) + amount;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

V8 - Integer Overflow
CRITICAL
Unchecked supply addition can overflow — total supply wraps to near-zero, making existing tokens appear ultra-rare. Unchecked amount addition allows account balance overflow. No authority check allows unauthorized minting.<|eot_id|>

    let mut mint = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodMint>::unpack(&mut mint_data)?;

fn pay_creator_fees<'a>(
    metadata_info: &AccountInfo<'a>, size: u64, is_native: bool,
) -> Result<u64, ProgramError> {
    let metadata = try_from_slice_unchecked::<Metadata>(&metadata_info.try_borrow_data()?)?;
    let fees = metadata.data.seller_fee_basis_points;
    let total_fee = (fees as u64) * size / 10000;
    let remaining_size = size - total_fee;

    match metadata.data.creators {
        Some(creators) => {
            for creator in creators {
                let creator_fee = creator.share as u64 * total_fee / 100;
                remaining_fee = remaining_fee - creator_fee;
            }
        }
        None => {}
    }
    Ok(remaining_size + remaining_fee)
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
HIGH
Unchecked fee * size can overflow for large trades with high basis points. Unchecked size - total_fee underflows if fees > 100%. Unchecked creator share accumulation can drain more than total. No metadata ownership check allows fake fee data.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced checked_mul (L295-297) with unchecked *, replaced checked_div (L298-299) with unchecked /, replaced checked_sub (L301-303,L313-315) with unchecked -, replaced checked_add (L396-398) with unchecked +, removed metadata owner check (L288-290)

pub fn process_delete(accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let name_account = next_account_info(accounts_iter)?;
    let name_owner = next_account_info(accounts_iter)?;
    let refund_target = next_account_info(accounts_iter)?;

    let name_record_header = NameRecordHeader::unpack_from_slice(&name_account.data.borrow())?;
    if !name_owner.is_signer || name_record_header.owner != *name_owner.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
    }

    let dest_amount: &mut u64 = &mut refund_target.lamports.borrow_mut();
    *dest_amount = *dest_amount + *source_amount;
    *source_amount = 0;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
MEDIUM
Unchecked lamport addition can overflow if destination has near-max lamports. Missing data zeroing leaves stale name record data — ghost accounts can be read by clients.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced saturating_add (L219) with unchecked +, removed data zeroing before close (L215)

pub fn process_cast_vote(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], vote: Vote,
) -> ProgramResult {
    voter_token_owner_record_data.unrelinquished_votes_count =
        voter_token_owner_record_data.unrelinquished_votes_count + 1;

    match &vote {
        Vote::Approve(choices) => {
            for (option, choice) in proposal_data.options.iter_mut().zip(choices) {
                option.vote_weight = option.vote_weight + choice.get_choice_weight(voter_weight)?;
            }
        }
        Vote::Deny => {
            proposal_data.deny_vote_weight = Some(
                proposal_data.deny_vote_weight.unwrap() + voter_weight);
        }
        Vote::Veto => {
            proposal_data.veto_vote_weight = proposal_data.veto_vote_weight + voter_weight;
        }
        _ => {}
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
HIGH
All vote weight accumulations use unchecked +. A whale voter can overflow any option's vote_weight, wrapping it to near-zero and effectively erasing all previous votes. Vote count overflow also possible.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced all checked_add calls with unchecked +: vote count (L109-111), approve weight (L120-122), deny weight (L128-131), veto weight (L133-135), removed Abstain error (L137-139)

fn process_refresh_reserve(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut reserve = Reserve::unpack(&reserve_info.data.borrow())?;
    if reserve_info.owner != program_id {
        return Err(LendingError::InvalidAccountOwner.into());
    }
    let clock = Clock::get()?;
    reserve.liquidity.update_borrow_rate(clock.slot)?;
    reserve.accrue_interest(clock.slot)?;
    reserve.last_update.mark_stale();

    let market_price = get_pyth_price(pyth_price_info, clock)?;
    reserve.liquidity.market_price = market_price;
    reserve.last_update.update_slot(clock.slot);
    Reserve::pack(reserve, &mut reserve_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Ownership check, slot-based rate update prevents stale rates, interest accrual with proper time tracking, oracle price validation via get_pyth_price.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept ownership check, rate update with slot, interest accrual, stale marking, oracle price fetch, slot update

fn process_cleanup_removed_validator(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    check_account_owner(stake_pool_info, program_id)?;
    let stake_pool = try_from_slice_unchecked::<StakePool>(&stake_pool_info.data.borrow())?;
    if !stake_pool.is_valid() {
        return Err(StakePoolError::InvalidState.into());
    }
    stake_pool.check_validator_list(validator_list_info)?;
    stake_pool.check_authority_withdraw(withdraw_authority_info.key, program_id, stake_pool_info.key)?;

    if validator.status != StakeStatus::DeactivatingAll
        && validator.status != StakeStatus::ReadyForRemoval {
        return Err(StakePoolError::WrongStakeStake.into());
    }
    if validator.transient_stake_lamports != 0 {
        return Err(StakePoolError::StakeLamportsNotEqualToMinimum.into());
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Status check prevents premature cleanup. Transient stake check ensures deactivation complete. Authority validation. Only DeactivatingAll or ReadyForRemoval validators can be cleaned up.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept ownership (L2156), validity (L2159-2161), validator list match (L2162), authority check (L2163-2166), status check (L2195-2200), transient lamports check (L2201-2204)

pub fn process_initialize_mint(
    accounts: &[AccountInfo], decimals: u8,
    mint_authority: &Pubkey, freeze_authority: COption<Pubkey>,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let rent = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let mut mint = Mint::unpack_unchecked(&mint_info.data.borrow())?;
    if mint.is_initialized {
        return Err(TokenError::AlreadyInUse.into());
    }
    if !Rent::from_account_info(rent)?.is_exempt(mint_info.lamports(), Mint::LEN) {
        return Err(TokenError::NotRentExempt.into());
    }

    mint.mint_authority = COption::Some(*mint_authority);
    mint.decimals = decimals;
    mint.is_initialized = true;
    mint.freeze_authority = freeze_authority;
    mint.supply = 0;

    Mint::pack(mint, &mut mint_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Re-initialization prevented (already initialized returns error). Rent exemption enforced prevents garbage-collectible mints. Supply starts at 0 — cannot be pre-inflated.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept is_initialized re-init prevention (L53-55), rent exemption check (L56-58), supply initialized to 0

pub fn process_initialize_mint(
    accounts: &[AccountInfo], decimals: u8,
    mint_authority: &Pubkey, freeze_authority: COption<Pubkey>,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let mut mint = Mint::unpack_unchecked(&mint_info.data.borrow())?;

    mint.mint_authority = COption::Some(*mint_authority);
    mint.decimals = decimals;
    mint.is_initialized = true;
    mint.freeze_authority = freeze_authority;
    Mint::pack(mint, &mut mint_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

pub fn process_freeze_account(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let mut source_account = Account::unpack(&source_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    source_account.state = AccountState::Frozen;
    Account::pack(source_account, &mut source_account_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

V1 - Access Control
CRITICAL
No freeze_authority check — anyone can freeze any account. No mint match — can freeze using wrong mint. Mints without freeze_authority can be frozen. Already-frozen check removed allows state confusion.<|eot_id|>

fn process_borrow_obligation_liquidity(
    program_id: &Pubkey, liquidity_amount: u64, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mut reserve = Reserve::unpack(&reserve_info.data.borrow())?;
    let mut obligation = Obligation::unpack(&obligation_info.data.borrow())?;
    let borrow_amount = reserve.calculate_borrow(liquidity_amount, clock.slot)?.borrow_amount;

    // WRONG: Transfer BEFORE recording borrow
    spl_token_transfer(TokenTransferParams {
        source: reserve_liquidity_supply_info.clone(),
        destination: destination_liquidity_info.clone(),
        amount: borrow_amount, authority: lending_market_authority_info.clone(),
        authority_signer_seeds, token_program: token_program_id.clone(),
    })?;

    // THEN update state — but funds already sent
    reserve.liquidity.borrow(borrow_amount)?;
    obligation.borrow_obligation_liquidity(borrow_amount, obligation_liquidity_index)?;
    Reserve::pack(reserve, &mut reserve_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Obligation::pack(obligation, &mut obligation_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
Funds transferred before borrow recorded. During transfer CPI, obligation still shows lower borrowed amount. Re-entry can borrow again since obligation limit not yet updated.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: spl_token_transfer (originally L1062-1068) placed before state updates reserve.borrow + obligation.borrow (originally L1033-1043)

pub fn process_withdraw_all_token_types(
    program_id: &Pubkey, pool_token_amount: u64, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let token_swap = SwapVersion::unpack(&swap_info.data.borrow())?;

    // WRONG: Transfer tokens out FIRST
    Self::token_transfer(swap_info.key, token_a_program_info.clone(),
        token_a_info.clone(), token_a_mint_info.clone(), dest_a_info.clone(),
        authority_info.clone(), token_swap.bump_seed(), token_a_amount, decimals_a)?;
    Self::token_transfer(swap_info.key, token_b_program_info.clone(),
        token_b_info.clone(), token_b_mint_info.clone(), dest_b_info.clone(),
        authority_info.clone(), token_swap.bump_seed(), token_b_amount, decimals_b)?;

    // THEN burn pool tokens — but user already has A and B
    Self::token_burn(swap_info.key, pool_token_program_info.clone(),
        source_info.clone(), pool_mint_info.clone(), authority_info.clone(),
        token_swap.bump_seed(), pool_token_amount)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
Tokens A and B transferred before pool tokens burned. During transfer CPI, pool tokens still exist. Attacker can re-enter withdraw, receiving double A and B before LP tokens are destroyed.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: token_transfer A and B (originally L838-854) placed before token_burn (originally L822-825)

Code:
RecordInstruction::Write { offset, data } => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    {
        let raw_data = &data_info.data.borrow();
        if raw_data.len() < RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX {
            return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
        }
        let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes::<RecordData>(
            &raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
        ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;
        if !account_data.is_initialized() {
            return Err(ProgramError::UninitializedAccount);
        }
        check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;
    }
    let start = RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX.saturating_add(offset as usize);
    let end = start.saturating_add(data.len());
    if end > data_info.data.borrow().len() {
        Err(ProgramError::AccountDataTooSmall)
    } else {
        data_info.data.borrow_mut()[start..end].copy_from_slice(data);
        Ok(())
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
No external CPI involved. Direct data copy with full bounds validation: size check, initialization check, authority check, saturating_add for offset, end > len bounds check.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction - no external CPI, direct data write with bounds check, authority validation, initialization check

fn check_pool_mint_address(
    program_id: &Pubkey, pool_key: &Pubkey, mint_key: &Pubkey,
) -> Result<u8, ProgramError> {
    let (expected_mint, bump) = crate::find_pool_mint_address(program_id, pool_key);
    if expected_mint != *mint_key {
        Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolMint.into())
    } else {
        Ok(bump)
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>
